verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Almagreira After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 14 kJ, 8 s period, lully 0/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully.
best.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 15 kJ, 8 s period, lully 0/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is usually too fat for exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia
Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push
